scene_summary: A corporate meeting where a manager (Ryan) is forced to explain why his absence will slow things down, while the rest of the team pretends this is a serious strategic concern.

signature_lines: despite being out of office. Ryan was kind enough to join us today just to quickly recalibrate on the potential downstream applications of his absence, right?
my unavailability will create some deceleration across my core work streams.
observations.interpersonal_note: the only one who actually acknowledges their own absence; everyone else treats it as a major crisis

signature_lines: And just from a sort of execution standpoint,
observations.interpersonal_note: tries to sound serious but keeps slipping into casual speech; clearly not buying the drama

signature_lines: that you being offline will produce the subsequent productivity delta for us to strategically neutralize.
I think I'm gonna line up here
observations.interpersonal_note: overly formal and trying too hard; their 'serious' analysis is just nonsense corporate jargon
always agrees with whatever's being said; no opinion of their own
interrupts and changes the subject mid-sentence; can't stay on topic for more than a second
